notes:
BW7 Boundaries Crossed (released 2012-11-07): 10 cards/pack, 36 packs/booster box. No Elite Trainer Box existed for this set — ETBs began with Plasma Storm (Feb 2013), so etbPacks=null and the only sealed product is the booster box (displayPacks=36). Confirmed via pittpokeresearch, collectorscache, Bulbapedia.

Pack composition follows the BW-era baseline: 5 common, 3 uncommon, 1 reverse-holo, 1 rare-or-better ("rare slot").

Rare-slot distribution is built from Flipside Gaming's era-average Actual Rarity figures (the only fetchable quantitative source; PokeGym and PokeBeach pull-rate threads were HTTP 403): Pokemon-EX ~1:18 packs -> ~2/box -> 0.056; Ultra Rare (Full Art) ~1:36 early BW improving toward ~1:18-24 in later sets, used ~1:24 -> ~1.5/box -> 0.042; Secret Rare ~1:72 packs -> ~0.5/box -> 0.014. Remaining 0.888 of the rare slot split rare-holo vs rare (~1/3 holo split typical of BW), giving rare-holo 0.300 and rare 0.588. Slot sums to exactly 1.0.

Ace Spec / Rare Ace cards (BW7 introduced 4) have no dedicated bucket in this set's available rarities; they are absorbed into the rare-holo/rare buckets per pokemontcg.io classification, so no separate distribution key is used.

Reverse-holo slot mapped across the set's reversible buckets (common/uncommon dominate, with small rare/rare-holo tail), summing to 1.0.

confidence=low: these are era-average rates applied without a published per-set study; granular BW7 community box-break threads exist but were not fetchable. EX/box (~2), FA/box (~1.5), secret/box (~0.5) are consistent with both the cited rates and the set's bucket counts (6 ex, 9 ultra-rare, 4 secret-rare). Vintage booster box carries a large sealed/collector premium, so EV/booster will sit well below the box's market price — expected, rates not inflated to compensate.
